design_philosophy.core_loop: Select country/track → race against AI opponents → earn money → upgrade car components → progress through 16 countries with 4 races each → unlock password saves
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing aggressive racing performance against vehicle damage management and fuel economy constraints; adapting to weather conditions (dry/wet tyres)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: International racing driver climbing a world championship ladder, customizing and tuning a competitive vehicle across exotic locations
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Mandatory upgrade system creates progression gating; fuel economy becomes critical late-game, forcing gearbox investment; damage system punishes reckless driving with spinouts; weather mechanics demand tactical tire changes

difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows resuming from any completed country
difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based checkpointing after each 4-race country completion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty levels (Amateur/Professional/Championship in US version, mapped to Easy/Normal/Hard in Japan). Players must finish in top 10 positions to qualify for next race. Customizable controls and gearshift (automatic/manual) available at opening menu.

progression.upgrade_system: Multi-component car upgrade system: Engine (acceleration, top speed, gear access), Nitro (boost strength/duration), Gearbox (top speed, fuel economy, acceleration), Armour (damage resistance by section), Tyres (grip/turning), Paint (cosmetic). Money earned per race spent on upgrades.
progression.unlock_mechanism: 64 tracks across 16 countries unlock sequentially; each country requires top-10 finishes in all 4 races to advance. Password system gates progression access.
replayability_hooks: Multiple difficulty levels affect opponent AI toughness and car handling
Weather conditions change per track, requiring strategy adjustments

world_building_techniques: 16 real-world countries as racing destinations (Australasia, Egypt, etc.)
Location-specific track design creates implicit geography
Notable landmark naming (Giza Necropolis renamed to Hugh Sitton due to naming rights agreement)
No explicit narrative; progression through world map is implicit world tour/championship

environmental_storytelling: Progressive country unlocks suggest ascending competitive ladder from Australasia to United States
Track-specific weather conditions (rain, snow) create regional atmosphere
Damage diagram visibility emphasizes consequence of aggressive racing
Car customization options allow player identity expression through vehicle tuning
